prose:
Fukui reactivity analysis was performed on acrolein (C4H4O, 8 atoms) using the fukui-reactivity skill with DFT at the B3LYP/def2-TZVP level (tier 'standard') in the gas phase, charge 0, multiplicity 1, via PySCF RKS (exact four-center integrals, no density fitting). The geometry was taken from the user-supplied .xyz file without re-optimization. Mulliken partial charges were computed for the neutral (N), cation (N-1, charge +1, doublet), and anion (N+1, charge -1, doublet) states; Fukui functions were derived from the charge differences.

Headline result: The most electrophilic site is the terminal β-carbon C4 with f⁺ = 0.64232102 and dual descriptor Δf = 0.55495. The most nucleophilic site is the carbonyl oxygen O1 with f⁻ = 0.39388616. All SCFs converged; integrity status = 'ok', trustworthy = true. Charge conservation: sum(f⁺) = 1.0000, sum(f⁻) = 1.0000 (both within tolerance). No engine warnings were reported.

Full per-atom results:
  Atom 1 (O):  f⁺=0.40136, f⁻=0.39389, f⁰=0.39763, Δf=+0.00748
  Atom 2 (C):  f⁺=0.02226, f⁻=0.00599, f⁰=0.01412, Δf=+0.01627
  Atom 3 (C):  f⁺=0.43405, f⁻=0.09140, f⁰=0.26272, Δf=+0.34264
  Atom 4 (C):  f⁺=0.64232, f⁻=0.08737, f⁰=0.36485, Δf=+0.55495 ← most electrophilic
  Atom 5 (H):  f⁺=−0.15726, f⁻=0.08596, f⁰=−0.03565, Δf=−0.24322
  Atom 6 (H):  f⁺=−0.20720, f⁻=0.18568, f⁰=−0.01076, Δf=−0.39288
  Atom 7 (H):  f⁺=−0.06212, f⁻=0.08449, f⁰=0.01118, Δf=−0.14660
  Atom 8 (H):  f⁺=−0.07341, f⁻=0.06523, f⁰=−0.00409, Δf=−0.13863

This confirms acrolein's 1,4-Michael addition reactivity: C4 is the dominant electrophilic (Michael-acceptor) site; O1 is the dominant nucleophilic site. CLI: chemkit_engine/cli.py fukui acrolein.xyz --method dft --tier standard --functional b3lyp --basis def2-tzvp --charge 0 --mult 1. Output: acrolein_fukui_dft.json.
